scope: Curated 4-track authority/measured benchmark batch assembled from existing local catalogs and existing local reports/manifests only.

summary.recommended_use: Batch together Atwood, KW51, TPU wind, and USGS NSMP to diversify authority/measured coverage across instrumented buildings, railway bridge response, and raw wind-tunnel mapping.

tracks.why_it_matters: It is the strongest local measured high-rise counterweight to the recurring MIDAS building file and already plugs into dynamic time-history, phase-correction, and noise-stress flows.

readiness.label: benchmarked_measured_holdout
readiness.summary: Local measured cases, benchmark KPIs, and noise convergence evidence already exist for the Atwood track.
signals: phase3_megastructure_pipeline_report.mgt_smoke2.json marks benchmark_pass=true and noise_convergence_pass=true for candidate_id zenodo_atwood_highrise_shm_2025.
hf_benchmark_report.mgt_smoke2.json passes KPI thresholds on open_data_measurement metrics.

direct_model_availability.label: no_local_direct_model
direct_model_availability.summary: Local evidence supports measured-case materialization and benchmarking, but not a track-native geometry adapter or direct structural model for Atwood.

optimization_availability.label: proxy_benchmark_available
optimization_availability.summary: Optimization-style benchmark evaluation is available through the materialized benchmark cases and top-k residual/meta KPI run, but it remains proxy benchmarking rather than direct track-native model optimization.

notes: The viewer entry is still registered/adapter pending, so local readiness is coming from measured-case and benchmark artifacts rather than a direct viewer/model bridge.
The Atwood-named conversion artifacts should be read together with the pipeline report, which is the safest local anchor for this track's current benchmark-ready state.

tracks.why_it_matters: It widens the benchmark family from instrumented buildings to full-scale railway bridge response and is the clearest local infrastructure-scale measured holdout.

readiness.label: materialized_but_benchmark_failing
readiness.summary: Measured cases and a source manifest are present locally, but the current KPI benchmark run fails badly and needs rematerialization or model-fit work before this track is batch-green.
signals: kw51_conversion_report.json and its source manifest pass contract and source-manifest checks with 99 benchmark cases.
commercial_benchmark_cases.kw51_railway_bridge.json is locally materialized but still carries unstable metrics and ATWOOD-style case IDs.

direct_model_availability.label: no_local_direct_model
direct_model_availability.summary: The local KW51 track is materialized as measured bridge-response cases only; there is no direct structural model or geometry adapter attached to it.

optimization_availability.label: not_currently_available
optimization_availability.summary: A benchmark pipeline exists, but the current local optimization-style KPI run is failing, so KW51 should not be treated as optimization-available today.

notes: Local materialization exists and is useful for authority/measured diversity, but the track is not yet safe to promote as a passing benchmark family.
The case ID pattern inside the current KW51 benchmark cases does not read like a clean KW51 naming pass, which is another reason to treat rematerialization as the next step.

tracks.why_it_matters: It is the best cataloged local route for diversifying measured building-response holdouts beyond Atwood by pairing structural and ground channels from an authority source.

readiness.label: registered_only_adapter_pending
readiness.summary: USGS NSMP is registered in the local catalogs and viewer placeholders, but there is no local materialized source drop, benchmark case set, or adapter yet.
signals: mega_structure_catalog.json lists usgs_nsmp_structural_arrays with dynamic-response pipeline fit.
benchmark_diversification_catalog.json recommends it for measured holdout expansion.
megastructure_usgs_nsmp_structural_arrays.json labels the track registered / adapter pending with no derived cases.

direct_model_availability.label: no_local_direct_model
direct_model_availability.summary: No direct model, geometry adapter, or local materialized structural-array package exists in the repo for USGS NSMP.

optimization_availability.label: not_available
optimization_availability.summary: Without a local materialized case family, there is no current optimization or benchmark execution path for USGS NSMP inside the repo.

notes: The next commands are exact repo command shapes, but they are future-facing and require a local USGS drop under implementation/phase1/open_data/usgs_nsmp before they can run successfully.
Current local evidence is registration-level only.

tracks.why_it_matters: It is the only local authority wind track with both isolated and interference high-rise cases, directly targeting the raw HFFB mapping diversity gap called out in the diversification catalog.

readiness.label: materialized_and_execution_complete_for_mapping
readiness.summary: Local TPU assets, gate evidence, and completed raw-mapping execution artifacts already exist for isolated and interference cases.
signals: tpu_hffb_benchmark_gate_report.json passes with two official TPU assets, one isolated and one interference case.
external_benchmark_execution_status_manifest.json records both TPU tasks as completed.
Per-case benchmark_task_result.json artifacts exist under release/external_benchmark_kickoff/runs/.

direct_model_availability.label: no_local_direct_model
direct_model_availability.summary: TPU is materialized as raw HFFB/pressure mapping input and execution artifacts, not as a direct structural model.

optimization_availability.label: mapping_ready_not_track_native_optimization_ready
optimization_availability.summary: The local TPU track is ready for raw mapping validation and external benchmark execution, but not for track-native structural optimization because wind time-history force columns are still incomplete and no direct model exists.

notes: The diversification catalog said TPU should be prioritized because wind raw mapping was green but source diversity was narrow; the local TPU execution artifacts show that the isolated/interference expansion has already started.
This track is ready for authority/measured batch inclusion as a wind mapping benchmark, not as a direct model benchmark.
